spoilers.title: #ONEPIECE1178 Well, from this point on, Oda couldn't make it any clearer; Sanji mentions way too often that it's "too easy." Their counterattack was enough. But as soon as Imu really arrives, it'll be
spoilers.text: #ONEPIECE1178 Well, from this point on, Oda couldn't make it any clearer; Sanji mentions way too often that it's "too easy." Their counterattack was enough. But as soon as Imu really arrives, it'll be over quickly. Get ready for some serious action... The Will of Marco (マルコの意志) (@TheWillOfMarco) #ONEPIECE1178 --OTHER SCENES IN ELBAPH IN THE CHAPTER-- (At the Fisherman Village) Giant: At any rate, just put out the fires! Giant: That bastard Loki defeated the monsters, but the cleanup is such a pain! (At the Hunter Village) Giant: They’re saying Loki didn’t actually kill King Harald... Giant: I feel bad for doubting him about that, but… look!! Even taking that into account, he’s still the same old troublesome guy as always!! (We see scenes of Straw Hats putting out the fires around Elbaph) Nami: Do your best with the rain, Zeus!!! Zeus: I will~~~!!! Jinbei: Sea Cloud... Shoulder Throw!!! Lilith: The ODC has its limits too, you know! Giant: There are no enemies in sight in the Craftsman Village, but there’s a fire!!! Giant: No enemies here in the Brewer’s Village, but there’s a fire! Giant: All that’s left is to extinguish the fires! Giant: Confirm the status and locations of the demons! (Note: referring to the three Knights of God) (At the The Owl Library) Robin: Saul! Saul: What’s the meaning of this?! (The fire inside the library is extinguished but the books and Biblo are nowhere to be found.) Saul: There’s no mistaking that the interior was once on fire, but… there’s not a single trace of burnt books… The shelves are all empty… Biblo the Owl isn’t here either…!! If we think optimistically, then… Robin: He took all the books? (Note: Robin used “彼” — kare, the Japanese pronoun for “he,” so Biblo is male. Not important I guess, but I just want to point it out.) Robin: If that’s the case, I’m so relieved… but where did Biblo go? (Scene cuts to Sanji and Franky side.) Sanji: Once the counterattack started, it ended pretty quickly… Franky: It’s not like we’re in an all-out war against some organization or anything!! Even if they’re knights of the “Holy Land,” there were only three of them. Still, they did a pretty good job of causing chaos in this massive land of Elbaph… Killingham: *What happened to Imu-sama!?* (At the Western Village) WHOAAAAAAAA!!! Brogy: Is it over?! Huff… Huff… Hajrudin: If they turn back to “white,” it’s a success… Dorry: If they regenerate, it’s not enough… (I guess turning back to "white" means turning back to "normal" in this context. Being in Domi Reversi = black. Note: this is just my personal deduction.) Giant: I’m sorry, Chiefs! I was swallowed by some kind of evil…! Zoro: It’s alright… we couldn’t resist it either. (LMAO 😂😂😂) DR Giant: Urgh… DR Giant: Damn the chiefs!!! DR Giant: How dare you…! Jarul: There are only two or three of them left... Zoro: Okay, I’ll handle thi— *Chopper arrives and slaps the remaining DR Giants from the back.* Chopper: YAH!!! Zoro: Chopper!! (Demon shadows comes out of the giants and they flipped back to normal.) Giant: Huh?! Chopper: Zoro~~~! Zoro: What did you just do?! Chopper: I have no idea, but… just by hitting them… I can cure their demonization! Zoro: Is that because you’re a “Great Doctor”? Chopper: EHHHH?! Even… even if you say that, it doesn’t make me happy at all, you bastard! <3 (Chopper says the line, but we can't see him doing the dance LOL) Brogy: No, that really must be the case!!! That’s amazing!!! Gabababa!!! Hajrudin: We’re all done here, but… what about the others? — https://nitter.net/TheWillOfMarco/status/2037181241997615603#m

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1178 Damn so Loki power has a nerf too like Luffy's one with G5? Or does he can´t use it because of his injuries? Seems like he can´t use his Dragon form for long. The Will of Marco (マルコの意志)
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1178 Damn so Loki power has a nerf too like Luffy's one with G5? Or does he can´t use it because of his injuries? Seems like he can´t use his Dragon form for long. The Will of Marco (マルコの意志) (@TheWillOfMarco) #ONEPIECE1178 --IMU VS. LUFFY & LOKI SCENE-- Imu: A few years ago… a ship manned by giants was spotted at sea. Its captain was said to be excessively massive. But those giants, moving while hidden in the deep fog… remained “unidentified,” continuing to target only the World Government. (Loki fires a lightning blast at Imu/Gunko) (BOOM!) (Luffy is returning to the battle after delivering Brook and Usopp to safety.) Luffy: Hey! I’m the one who’s gonna send her flying!!! (Imu/Gunko reappears overhead. Seems she dodged the blast.) Imu: —Meanwhile, at the same time… reports came in of a giant flying creature being sighted. Loki: You dodged that?! You’re way too small! Where are you?! Imu: So the rumors were true, after all… I see… now it all makes sense… (So basically, Loki was doing what Harald did when he served the World Government, but Loki’s targets are WG-affiliated, not pirates.) Luffy: There you are!! How dare you do that to Brook… and Usopp!! (All of a sudden, Imu’s shadowy form emerges from Gunko.) Luffy: WHAT?! (Luffy’s eye pops out, G5 style, LOL) Luffy: Something came out!! Imu: If only thou wouldst vanish from this world… just like Rocks!!! A KUWARU!!! (Demon Covenant) Luffy: Wah! Loki: Hm?! Imu: In exchange for a fixed measure of thy lifespan, I shall grant thee greater strength… and in return… ye two… shall hate one another! DOMI REVERSI!!! (THUMP! THUMP!) (Imu pierces Loki and Luffy with shadow tentacles, just like that time with Rocks, but…) Luffy and Loki: .............!! (The Domi Reversi has no effect on them. Loki snaps the shadow tentacle, and Luffy kicks it, LOL.) Luffy: What’s with this thing?! It doesn’t even hurt! Loki: So annoying! Hey you! Did you just mention Rocks?! Imu: *As expected… it would seem that thou two art…* Luffy: Gomu-Gomu no…!!! Loki: The target’s too small—I can’t fight! ZWOOM! (Loki shrinks back to his normal size—his normal Ancient Giant form.) Luffy: DAWN GATLING!!! Loki: Ragnir!! (Squirrel Ragnir transforms back into a hammer. This time, the hammer still has squirrel features—face and ears. Looks cute, LOL.) Ragnir: Kuriri!!! Loki: Move out of the way, Luffy!!! (Yo, is this the first time Loki called Luffy by his name?) Ragnir: Kuriri!!! Luffy: !! Whoa! Wait, wait!!! Imu: ....!!! Loki: NIBELHEIM!!! (NIFLHEIM!!) (Loki strikes Imu/Gunko, encasing them in a gigantic block of ice.) CRACKLE! CRACKLE! Luffy: WAHH!!! Is that how you finish them off?! I’m not done punching her yet… Fuheeeh… (Luffy’s Gear 5 wears off. He returns to his withered old man form; his clothes revert to Elbaph-style attire. He falls down.) PLOP! Loki: I don’t know who you are, but… this is quicker… I’ve been gigantic for too long. Huff… Huff… (Seems Loki gets fatigued when using Nidhogg form.) — https://nitter.net/TheWillOfMarco/status/2037171701726011476#m

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1178 At the end of the chapter, Imu said: "Gorosei... Mu will, for a while... leave the Holy Land..." (This follows the original sentence flow in Japanese.) For better flow in English: "Goros
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1178 At the end of the chapter, Imu said: "Gorosei... Mu will, for a while... leave the Holy Land..." (This follows the original sentence flow in Japanese.) For better flow in English: "Gorosei... For a while, Mu will... leave the Holy Land..." What's interesting about this line is that the Japanese term Imu used for "leave" is "空ける" (akeru), which is a different writing of the verb "開ける", with one of the term's meanings is "to leave temporarily." Now, the kanji "空" used in "空ける" (akeru), in itself, means empty, sky, void, vacant, or vacuum. Another interesting aspect is its outdated kanji form, "虚", which also means void or empty. This outdated (or archaic if you will) form is used in "The Empty Throne" (虚の玉座, kara no gyokuza). Personally, I think it’s a beautiful piece of symbolism: with just one kanji, Oda is emphasizing that "The Empty Throne" will truly be "empty" for a while during Imu's absence in Pangaea Castle. 🤯

items.text: #ONEPIECE1178 At the end of the chapter, Imu said: "Gorosei... Mu will, for a while... leave the Holy Land..." (This follows the original sentence flow in Japanese.) For better flow in English: "Gorosei... For a while, Mu will... leave the Holy Land..." What's interesting about this line is that the Japanese term Imu used for "leave" is "空ける" (akeru), which is a different writing of the verb "開ける", with one of the term's meanings is "to leave temporarily." Now, the kanji "空" used in "空ける" (akeru), in itself, means empty, sky, void, vacant, or vacuum. Another interesting aspect is its outdated kanji form, "虚", which also means void or empty. This outdated (or archaic if you will) form is used in "The Empty Throne" (虚の玉座, kara no gyokuza). Personally, I think it’s a beautiful piece of symbolism: with just one kanji, Oda is emphasizing that "The Empty Throne" will truly be "empty" for a while during Imu's absence in Pangaea Castle. 🤯
